scenes.location: Creator studio building exterior, Austin Tech District
scenes.visual_description: A modern glass-and-steel studio building on a mixed-use tech corridor. Police tape blocks off the entrance while two patrol cars sit curbside. The pavement looks damp and reflects neon from nearby storefronts.
scenes.lighting: Cool dawn ambient light mixed with red-blue police light reflections
scenes.atmosphere: Public-facing and tense; a place built for cameras now framed as a crime scene.

scenes.location: Interior suite layout: streaming studio + adjacent control room
scenes.visual_description: A compact two-room production suite: the main streaming studio opens into a small control room packed with monitors and network gear. A side entrance corridor leads directly into the control room, and a single interior door connects control room to studio.
scenes.time_of_day: late night (context: the 2:47 AM outage window)
scenes.lighting: Cool LED spill from monitor screens and dim overhead practicals
scenes.atmosphere: Claustrophobic and technical; the kind of space where a small action can cause a huge outage.

scenes.title: Streaming Studio — Interior Setup (Pre-Collapse)
scenes.visual_description: Inside the soundproofed creator studio: RGB accent lights, acoustic foam panels, a desk with a high-end PC tower, dual monitors, a camera on tripod aimed at a gaming chair, a boom mic, energy drink cans, and a small mini-fridge in the corner. A soft key light faces the chair; cables are neatly routed. It feels clean, curated, and claustrophobic.

scenes.title: Parking Lot Outside the Studio — Tyler Watching the Stream
scenes.visual_description: A dim Austin tech-district parking lot at night: rows of cars, a few flickering LED lot lights, the glass studio building in the background. Inside one parked car, the glow of a phone/laptop screen reflects on a young man’s face as he watches the livestream; hoodie up, anxious posture. Rain-slick pavement and distant neon signage add tension.

scenes.title: Control Room — Network Rack and Switching Gear
scenes.visual_description: Adjacent control room packed with production gear: a small network rack with a router, switch, labeled ethernet cables, a streaming encoder box, and a monitor showing the livestream dashboard. A side entrance door is visible. The room is darker and more utilitarian than the studio, with harsh overhead lighting and a faint hum of equipment.
